title: Address CodeRabbit PR 22 feedback
description: Address unresolved CodeRabbit feedback on PR 22 by pricing fallback usage when stream consumption is skipped.
acceptance_criteria: Fallback iteration accumulator prices result usage when a model is available; regression test covers no-consume fallback pricing; checks pass; CodeRabbit thread is replied to and resolved.

title: Fix PR 22 quality gate findings
description: Address quality-gate findings on PR 22: redact structured log stream payloads, log real iteration outcomes, cover early failure observability, and make completion regex matching deterministic.
acceptance_criteria: Structured stream logs redact sensitive text/tool inputs or otherwise avoid raw payload persistence; iteration_end logs real outcome and exitCode for success and failure paths; early crash/no-consume paths still write iteration_end; global/sticky completion regexes are deterministic; tests, typecheck, lint, and build pass; fixes are pushed to PR 22.

title: Resolve PR 22 merge conflicts
description: Resolve current main conflicts on PR 22 and validate the branch before pushing.
acceptance_criteria: PR 22 branch merges current main without conflicts; tests and typecheck pass; resolution commit is pushed to the PR branch.

title: Resolve PR 22 merge conflicts
description: Resolve current main conflicts on PR 22 and validate the branch before pushing.
acceptance_criteria: PR 22 branch merges current main without conflicts; tests and typecheck pass; resolution commit is pushed to the PR branch.

title: Add Codex pricing support to PR 22
description: PR 22 adds model-agnostic cost calculation but bundled pricing only covers Claude models. Add OpenAI Codex model pricing and usage compatibility tests so Codex runs produce non-zero costs when known models emit compatible token usage fields.
acceptance_criteria: Known Codex/OpenAI model IDs in bundled pricing calculate non-zero costs from compatible usage events; unknown models still warn once and remain structurally safe; tests cover OpenAI Responses-style usage fields including cached input tokens.

title: Ralph v1: TypeScript CLI rewrite
description:
# Ralph v1: TypeScript CLI rewrite

## Problem Statement

The current Ralph workflow (`afk-ralph.sh`, `ralph-once.sh`) is a pair of bash scripts that runs `claude -p` in a loop against `scripts/ralph/prompt.md`. It's served its purpose but is slow, gives almost no visibility into what the agent is doing, can't reliably notify on completion with useful context (just plain text "done"), has no PR link in the notification, only supports Claude (not Codex), can't be killed cleanly, has no cost/token accounting, and is implemented as bash which is hard to test and inflexible. There's a redundant HITL mode (`ralph-once`) that's never used. The user wants a proper TypeScript CLI: well-typed, testable, agent-agnostic (swap Claude/Codex with same orchestration), with real-time stream rendering, draft-PR-then-ready workflow, rich WhatsApp notify with a PR link, opt-in observability hooks, clean shutdown, and a small lean prompt that pre-fills context via shell expressions rather than verbose instructions.

## Solution

Replace the bash with a TypeScript CLI named `ralph`. Single binary, single command surface (`ralph init`, `ralph run`, `ralph status`, `ralph tail`, `ralph stop`). One `ralph run` invocation produces one semantically-named source branch, one draft PR (opened after iteration 1), and one rich WhatsApp message at terminal state (COMPLETE or stall). Each iteration spawns a fresh agent subprocess (Claude Code or Codex) inside a worktree, parses its stream-JSON into normalised events, renders streamed prose + iteration summary boxes in the terminal, and tracks token + cost. A single quality-gate agent invocation runs at COMPLETE on the full PR diff: reviews, auto-fixes high-severity findings, files beads for ambiguous follow-ups, and writes the PR title + 2-sentence body before marking the PR ready. The CLI subprocess-wraps each agent — no Claude Agent SDK dependency — so adding Codex/Qwen/Kimi later is symmetrical. The whole thing is daemonisable (`ralph run --detach`) for true AFK use, with state JSON under `.ralph/state/<pid>.json` driving `ralph status` / `tail` / `stop`. Beads is the only tracker.

## User Stories

1. As a developer running Ralph AFK, I want to invoke a single `ralph run --branch feat/whatsapp-rich-notify` command, so that I can walk away without further input and trust the agent to ship a PR.
2. As a developer, I want the new CLI to be installed via npm so that I get a globally available `ralph` binary without symlinking bash scripts manually.
3. As a developer setting up Ralph in a new project, I want to run `ralph init` and have it scaffold `.ralph/` (config + env example + prompt template + gitignore entries), so that I don't need a separate skill to bootstrap.
4. As a developer, I want `ralph init` to write a default `.ralph/prompt.md` and open it in `$EDITOR`, so that I can review and tailor the prompt before my first run.
5. As a developer, I want to pick between Claude Code and Codex via `--agent` flag, so that I can swap cheaper or different models without changing my project setup.
6. As a developer, I want both agents (Claude and Codex) to be invoked with their equivalents of `--dangerously-skip-permissions`, so that the agent never blocks waiting for approval mid-run.
7. As a developer, I want each iteration to start with a fresh agent context, so that prompt-cache rot and conversation drift don't degrade quality over a long invocation.
8. As a developer, I want the prompt to be lean (~25 lines) and pre-fill its context via `!` shell expressions (`bd ready`, `bd list --type epic`, `bd comments`, `bd memories`, `git log <base>..HEAD`, `.claude/rules/*`), so that the agent sees current state at the top without re-reading static workflow instructions every iteration.
9. As a developer, I want the prompt to instruct the agent to use the `/tdd` skill, so that the implementation follows the red-green-refactor pattern I've already defined.
10. As a developer, I want the prompt to refer to project checks as "feedback loop", so that the language matches the rest of my workflow.
11. As a developer, I want the prompt to include a light hint about subagents (use them for parallelisable subtasks), so that the agent uses them when appropriate without burning tokens on trivial work.
12. As a developer, I want each iteration to commit a one-line note to the active epic, so that history stays scannable and detail lives in commit messages where it belongs.
13. As a developer, I want to see the agent's text stream in real time in my terminal with dimmed styling, so that I have visibility into what's happening without parsing raw JSON.
14. As a developer, I want tool calls to render as one-line entries (e.g. `🔧 Bash: bd ready ...`) with truncated args, so that the stream stays scannable.
15. As a developer, I want a styled summary box between iterations showing iteration number, task closed, tokens used, and cost incurred, so that I can track progress at a glance.
16. As a developer, I want a final summary box at exit (COMPLETE or stall) with totals, so that I see the run outcome without scrolling back.
17. As a developer running AFK, I want to receive a rich WhatsApp message on COMPLETE or stall via CallMeBot, so that I get notified the moment my run ends with all the context I need.
18. As a developer, I want the WhatsApp message to include status (✅/⚠️), project name, branch, PR URL, iterations used vs max, wall-clock time, tasks done/blocked counts, tokens, cost, and a brief one-line summary per task completed plus the QG findings line, so that I can decide whether to context-switch without opening my laptop.
19. As a developer, I want the WhatsApp message to be best-effort — if CallMeBot fails or times out, the run still completes successfully, so that an external service outage doesn't poison my run.
20. As a developer, I want WhatsApp notify to be opt-in via `.ralph/.env`, so that projects without CallMeBot credentials silently skip notify.
21. As a developer, I want the WhatsApp message to be truncated gracefully when very long task lists would exceed CallMeBot's URL length cap, so that the message still gets through with a "+ N more" indicator.
22. As a developer, I want `ralph run --branch feat/x` to create a worktree under `.ralph/worktrees/feat-x/`, work in it, and clean up on exit, so that my host repo stays clean.
23. As a developer, I want `--branch` to be a required flag following semantic prefixes (`feat/`, `fix/`, `chore/`, `docs/`, `refactor/`, `perf/`, `test/`, `style/`), so that branches are consistent and lintable.
24. As a developer, I want ralph to open a draft PR after iteration 1's first push to origin, so that I can monitor progress on GitHub in parallel to the terminal.
25. As a developer, I want subsequent iterations to push commits to the same draft PR, so that I get one consolidated PR with multiple commits per vertical slice.
26. As a developer, I want the quality gate to run once at COMPLETE on the full PR diff (not per iteration), so that I pay for ~1 extra agent invocation per run and the reviewer can spot cross-iteration drift.
27. As a developer, I want the quality gate to auto-fix high-severity findings in place and file beads for ambiguous decisions, so that my PR is ready-to-merge wherever possible and follow-ups are queued for me.
28. As a developer, I want the quality gate to write the PR's title and 2-sentence what/why body based on the full diff, so that the PR reads like a human-authored one without me intervening.
29. As a developer, I want the PR title to use the branch's semantic prefix (e.g. `feat: WhatsApp rich notify`) and the body to be brief (2 sentences + bullet list of beads closed) with no Ralph branding or run metadata, so that reviewers see a clean diff.
30. As a developer, I want the quality gate to use my existing `/quality-gate` skill (which wraps `code-quality-verifier`), so that I don't maintain a duplicate review prompt.
31. As a developer, I want the quality gate's failures (ambiguous findings) to become open beads with the active epic as parent and a follow-up label, so that I find them next time I run Ralph.
32. As a developer running on a stall (max-iter without COMPLETE), I want the PR to stay draft and the WhatsApp message to warn me, so that I can pick up where Ralph stopped.
33. As a developer, I want a default max-iter of 10, so that a runaway agent costs at most ~10 iterations before I get pinged.
34. As a developer, I want a per-iteration timeout of 30 minutes, so that a hung agent process can't burn cost indefinitely.
35. As a developer, I want to override max-iter and timeout via CLI flags, so that long-running epics aren't artificially capped.
36. As a developer, I want first Ctrl-C to send SIGTERM to the agent subprocess, drain its stream up to 30 seconds, print a partial summary, and exit with code 130, so that I can stop cleanly.
37. As a developer, I want second Ctrl-C within 5 seconds to send SIGKILL and exit immediately, so that a stuck child process doesn't trap me.
38. As a developer, I want the PR to remain draft after Ctrl-C, so that partial work is still inspectable on GitHub.
39. As a developer, I want to run ralph in detached mode (`ralph run --detach`), so that I can close my terminal and walk away.
40. As a developer, I want `ralph status` to show all active runs (PID, branch, current iteration, current task, cost-so-far), so that I can check on background runs.
41. As a developer, I want `ralph tail [<pid>]` to follow the log file of a running invocation, so that I can debug what's happening without restarting.
42. As a developer, I want `ralph stop [<pid>]` to send SIGTERM to a detached run, so that I can cancel it gracefully.
43. As a developer, I want each invocation to have its own state file under `.ralph/state/<pid>.json`, so that multiple parallel runs are tracked independently.
44. As a developer, I want state files removed on clean exit, so that `ralph status` only shows actually-running invocations.
45. As a developer, I want stale state files (PID no longer alive) to be detected and cleaned up by `ralph status`, so that crashes don't leave phantom entries.
46. As a developer, I want logs always written to `.ralph/logs/<timestamp>-<pid>.log`, so that I can grep run history even when I wasn't watching live.
47. As a developer, I want logs to be append-only structured (one JSON-line per event), so that I can write tooling against them later.
48. As a developer, I want token usage parsed per iteration from the agent's stream-JSON, so that cost tracking is accurate per agent without an extra API call.
49. As a developer, I want cost calculated against a shipped pricing table (per model, per token type: input / output / cache-create / cache-read), so that I see USD totals in the iteration summary and final summary.
50. As a developer, I want a custom completion signal via `--complete-signal '<promise>SHIPPED</promise>'`, so that I can repurpose ralph for non-default semantics without editing the prompt.
51. As a developer, I want the default completion signal to remain `<promise>COMPLETE</promise>`, so that my existing prompts continue to work.
52. As a developer, I want project config in `.ralph/ralph.config.json` (default agent, default model, max-iter, branch prefix list, completion signal, feedback-loop discovery override), so that I set these once per project and skip flags.
53. As a developer, I want secrets in `.ralph/.env` (CallMeBot phone + apikey, agent API keys), so that secrets aren't in version control while config is.
54. As a developer, I want `ralph init` to update `.gitignore` to exclude `.ralph/.env`, `.ralph/state/`, `.ralph/logs/`, `.ralph/worktrees/`, so that I don't accidentally commit secrets or transient state.
55. As a developer, I want CLI flags to override `.ralph/ralph.config.json` values, so that per-run overrides don't require editing files.
56. As a developer, I want the prompt to support sandcastle-style `{{KEY}}` substitution with built-ins `{{BRANCH}}` and `{{TARGET_BRANCH}}`, so that the agent always knows which branch to push to.
57. As a developer, I want the prompt to support `` !`shell command` `` expressions, so that I can pre-fill any current state I want at the top of each iteration.
58. As a developer, I want unmatched `{{KEY}}` in the prompt to error out before iteration 1, so that typos surface fast.
59. As a developer, I want shell-expression failures to be visible (not silent) so that I can debug a broken pre-fill expression.
60. As a developer, I want ralph to only operate against beads (not GitHub Issues), so that the CLI surface stays minimal and the prompt template is single-purpose.
61. As a developer, I want the prompt's stop condition to be empty `bd ready --parent <active-epic>`, so that completion is automatic rather than agent-judged.
62. As a maintainer of the ralph repo, I want the bash scripts replaced (not coexisting), so that the repo's surface area is one CLI not two.
63. As a maintainer, I want sandcastle's `AgentProvider`, `PromptPreprocessor`, `WorktreeManager`, and sync-in/out patterns lifted into this codebase, so that I get battle-tested patterns without depending on sandcastle's release cadence.
64. As a maintainer, I want all deep modules (modules 1-11 in the breakdown) covered by unit tests with fixture inputs, so that regressions surface in CI before they ship.
65. As a maintainer, I want stream-JSON parsing in `AgentProvider` covered by per-agent contract tests (Claude fixtures + Codex fixtures), so that a CLI upgrade to either agent surfaces format drift immediately.
66. As a maintainer, I want the `ParsedStreamEvent` type to be the single normalised event shape across all agents, so that downstream consumers (StreamDisplay, CompletionDetector, CostCalculator) don't branch on agent identity.
67. As a maintainer, I want the project structured with deep modules behind narrow interfaces, so that adding a new agent (Qwen, Kimi) or sandbox provider (Docker) later is symmetric to the existing patterns.

## Implementation Decisions

### Repository

- The contents of `ralph-wiggum-claude` are replaced by the TypeScript CLI. Bash scripts and old templates are deleted on the new branch; history preserves them. The repo *becomes* the CLI.
- The npm publishing strategy is unresolved (whether to publish as `ralph` or a scoped name); the package metadata is filled in once decided. This does not block implementation.

### Architecture

- The CLI subprocess-wraps each agent CLI; **no Claude Agent SDK dependency**. Recorded as ADR-0001.
- The architecture lifts sandcastle's `AgentProvider`, `PromptPreprocessor`, `WorktreeManager`, sync-in/out, and branch-strategy mechanics. The implementations are re-implemented in this repo (not imported from sandcastle) to avoid an upstream coupling.
- One source branch + one PR per `ralph` invocation. Recorded as ADR-0002.

### Modules

The implementation defines the following modules. Deep modules listed first.

**Deep modules (testable in isolation):**

- `AgentProvider` — pluggable shim per agent CLI. Interface: `name: string`, `env: Record<string,string>`, `buildPrintCommand(options: AgentCommandOptions): PrintCommand`, `parseStreamLine(line: string): ParsedStreamEvent[]`, `parseSessionUsage?(content: string): IterationUsage | undefined`. Two concrete implementations: `claude(model)` and `codex(model, options?)`. `ParsedStreamEvent` is normalised: `text | result | tool_call | session_id`.
- `PromptPreprocessor` — pure transformation. Reads `.ralph/prompt.md`, runs `{{KEY}}` substitution first, then `!` shell-expression expansion. Built-in keys (`BRANCH`, `TARGET_BRANCH`) cannot be overridden. Unmatched `{{KEY}}` is a fatal error.
- `WorktreeManager` — owns `.ralph/worktrees/<slugified-branch>/`. Creates a git worktree, attaches branch, cleans up on success or failure. Used by all branch strategies except `head`.
- `StateStore` — owns `.ralph/state/<pid>.json` lifecycle. Atomic updates (write-rename pattern). Lists active states, detects stale PIDs, removes them on clean exit.
- `CostCalculator` — pricing table per model × per-token type (input / output / cache-create / cache-read). Pricing data shipped in a versioned JSON inside the package. Takes `IterationUsage` events, returns dollar amounts. Per-iteration and cumulative.
- `WhatsAppNotifier` — formats the rich plain-text message; URL-encodes; hits CallMeBot with a 10-second timeout; best-effort (failures logged but never throw upstream). Truncates task list to first N entries with a "+ M more" tail when over URL length budget.
- `Daemonizer` — single function: takes a command + log path, spawns a detached child via `child_process.spawn({detached: true, stdio: 'ignore'})`, returns the PID.
- `QualityGateRunner` — orchestrates the QG agent invocation. Builds the QG prompt (inputs: the PR diff via `git diff <base>..HEAD`, the list of touched beads, the active epic's notes, `.claude/rules/*` and project conventions). Spawns via `AgentProvider`. Captures structured output: PR title, PR body (2-sentence summary + bullet task list), filed-bead IDs.
- `ConfigLoader` — loads `.ralph/ralph.config.json` + `.ralph/.env`, merges with CLI flags (CLI wins), validates against a Zod schema. Surfaces validation errors with full path + suggestion.
- `CompletionDetector` — buffered scan over `ParsedStreamEvent`s for the completion-signal regex. Default `<promise>COMPLETE</promise>`. Configurable per-run.
- `BranchPRManager` — wraps `gh` CLI. Creates draft PR after first push (`gh pr create --draft --base <target> --head <source>`). Edits title/body on QG completion (`gh pr edit`). Marks ready (`gh pr ready`). Detects pre-existing PR on re-run and skips re-creation.

**Medium modules:**

- `StreamDisplay` — renders `ParsedStreamEvent` to terminal. Streamed prose dimmed; tool calls one-line; iteration summary boxes between iterations; final summary at exit. Uses `@clack/prompts` for styled output. Also writes structured (one JSON-per-line) log to `.ralph/logs/`.
- `InitWizard` — `ralph init` flow: prompts for default agent, default model, max-iter, branch prefix list, beads ID prefix. Writes `.ralph/ralph.config.json`, `.ralph/.env.example`, `.ralph/prompt.md` (from bundled template, with `{{KEY}}` placeholders filled in), updates `.gitignore`. Opens `.ralph/prompt.md` in `$EDITOR` at the end.

**Shallow modules:**

- `Orchestrator` — the iteration loop. Wires the other modules. Handles SIGINT (soft → hard), per-iteration timeout (30 min default), max-iter exhaustion, completion-signal detection, quality-gate invocation at COMPLETE, WhatsApp notify on terminal state.
- `CLI` — argument parsing + dispatch. Sub-commands: `ralph init`, `ralph run`, `ralph status`, `ralph tail`, `ralph stop`. Argument-parsing library: `@effect/cli` if Effect feels native, else `commander`. Decision deferred to implementation slice.

### Prompt template

The bundled `.ralph/prompt.md` template (written by `ralph init`) is sandcastle-style: terse numbered workflow, context pre-fill via shell expressions, completion signal at the bottom. The current draft is:

```markdown
# Context

!`bd ready --type task --limit 20`
!`bd list --type epic --status open`
!`bd comments $(bd list --type epic --json | jq -r '.[0].id')`
!`bd memories`
!`git log {{TARGET_BRANCH}}..HEAD --oneline -10`
!`find .claude/rules -type f -exec echo '--- {} ---' \; -exec cat {} \;`

# Task

You are Ralph. Pick the highest-priority ready bead, lock it,
implement it, commit, push. One task per iteration.

## Workflow

1. Claim the top `bd ready` task atomically (`bd update <id> --claim`)
2. Read the bead (`bd show <id>`) and the epic's notes
3. Implement with `/tdd` — red → green → refactor
4. Run the project's feedback loop (discover from CLAUDE.md, package.json, pyproject.toml, Makefile)
5. Commit, push to {{BRANCH}}
6. Close the bead (`bd close <id>`), append a one-line iteration note to the epic

## Rules

- One task per iteration. Don't batch.
- Don't commit if the feedback loop fails.
- Don't close a task you didn't ship.
- Use subagents for parallelisable subtasks.

# Done

When `bd ready --parent <epic>` returns empty, output
`<promise>COMPLETE</promise>`.
```

This is the *template*; users edit it freely. The shell expressions for `bd memories` and `.claude/rules/*` may be tightened by the implementer if they produce excessive output (cap length, summarise).

### PR lifecycle

- After iteration 1's first commit push: `gh pr create --draft --base <target> --head <source>` with a placeholder title (branch name) and a placeholder body (`Generated by Ralph — in progress`).
- Iterations 2..N: just `git push`. No PR API calls.
- On COMPLETE: `QualityGateRunner` produces final title + body. Orchestrator calls `gh pr edit --title <X> --body <Y>` then `gh pr ready`.
- On stall: PR stays draft. WhatsApp warns.
- On Ctrl-C: PR stays draft. State file removed. No WhatsApp.

### WhatsApp message format

Plain text (CallMeBot can't render markdown). Example for COMPLETE:

```
Ralph ✅ COMPLETE
feat/whatsapp-rich-notify · ralph-wiggum-claude

PR: https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/pull/<n>
Iterations: 7/10 · 23m
Tasks: 7 done, 0 blocked
Tokens: 1.2M in / 180k out · Cost: $4.12

Done:
- rwc-12: <bead title>
- rwc-13: <bead title>
- ...

QG: 2 issues filed (rwc-23, rwc-24)
```

For stall the status line becomes `Ralph ⚠️ STALLED` and the iteration line shows max reached.

Truncation: if the URL-encoded message exceeds CallMeBot's safe budget (~1500 chars), truncate the `Done:` list to the first 10 entries plus a `- +N more` tail.

### Defaults

- `max-iter`: 10
- per-iteration timeout: 30 min
- agent: `claude` (model `sonnet`, latest)
- branch strategy: `branch` (named branch, no auto-merge)
- completion signal: `<promise>COMPLETE</promise>`
- output: streamed prose + iteration summary boxes (terminal) + structured log file (always)
- WhatsApp: opt-in via env (`WHATSAPP_PHONE`, `WHATSAPP_APIKEY` in `.ralph/.env`)

### CLI surface

- `ralph init` — interactive wizard.
- `ralph run --branch <name> [--milestone <m>] [--instructions <text>] [--agent <claude|codex>] [--model <m>] [--max-iter <n>] [--timeout-min <n>] [--complete-signal <regex>] [--detach]`
- `ralph status` — lists active runs (reads `.ralph/state/*.json`).
- `ralph tail [<pid>]` — follows the log file. If no PID, picks the most recent.
- `ralph stop [<pid>]` — SIGTERM to PID. If no PID, picks the only active run (errors if multiple).

### Iteration error handling

- Agent crashes (non-zero exit): treat as iteration failure, log, move to next iteration. Cumulative crash count tracked; if >50% of iterations crash, abort the invocation with a stall.
- Agent timeout (30 min wall clock): SIGTERM agent, drain, move to next iteration.
- `gh` CLI errors during PR creation: log, retry once, continue (the PR can be created manually if it fails twice). Subsequent iterations push commits regardless.

### State file shape

```json
{
  "pid": 12345,
  "branch": "feat/whatsapp-rich-notify",
  "agent": "claude",
  "model": "sonnet",
  "startedAt": "2026-05-21T10:00:00Z",
  "iteration": { "current": 3, "max": 10 },
  "currentBead": "rwc-14",
  "tasksDone": ["rwc-12", "rwc-13"],
  "tokens": { "input": 312000, "output": 48000 },
  "costUsd": 1.23,
  "logPath": ".ralph/logs/2026-05-21T10-00-00-12345.log",
  "prUrl": "https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/pull/<n>"
}
```

## Testing Decisions

Good tests verify external behaviour, not implementation details. Stream parsing is tested against captured fixture JSON, not by mocking internal state. The Orchestrator is tested with a fake `AgentProvider` that emits scripted streams, not by mocking `child_process.spawn`. WhatsApp notify is tested by passing a fake `fetch` implementation, not by reading internal HTTP state.

Tests run via `vitest`. Coverage targets all deep modules (1–11 in the breakdown):

- **`AgentProvider`**: per-agent fixture contract tests. Capture a real `claude -p --output-format stream-json --verbose` and a real `codex exec --json` invocation against a tiny prompt, save the resulting stream as fixture files, and contract-test that `parseStreamLine` produces the expected `ParsedStreamEvent[]` for each fixture. Edge cases: malformed JSON lines (skip silently), partial streams (no completion event), tool calls with missing required fields (skip the tool call).
- **`PromptPreprocessor`**: unit tests for `{{KEY}}` substitution (missing key fatal; unused key warning), built-in keys not overridable, shell-expression expansion (success path + failure surfaces).
- **`WorktreeManager`**: integration tests against a temp git repo — create, branch attach, cleanup, cleanup-on-failure.
- **`StateStore`**: atomic-write tests, stale-PID detection, list-and-read multi-run scenarios.
- **`CostCalculator`**: pricing-table lookups per model + token type, cumulative aggregation, unknown-model fallback (warn + zero cost).
- **`WhatsAppNotifier`**: message formatting (COMPLETE + stall variants), URL truncation at the budget, network-failure swallowing.
- **`Daemonizer`**: smoke test that the spawned child outlives the parent and writes to the log path.
- **`QualityGateRunner`**: contract test against a fake `AgentProvider` that returns a scripted QG response (PR title + body + filed beads); verify the orchestrator wires the response back into `BranchPRManager` correctly.
- **`ConfigLoader`**: schema validation (valid + invalid configs), CLI-flag override precedence, secret-file isolation.
- **`CompletionDetector`**: regex detection across stream chunks (events split across multiple ParsedStreamEvent boundaries), custom-signal override, no-match scenario.
- **`BranchPRManager`**: tests around `gh` CLI invocation patterns using a fake shell-exec; happy path, pre-existing PR, edit + ready transition.

Prior art: sandcastle's test suite is the closest pattern — see `AgentProvider.test.ts`, `PromptPreprocessor.test.ts`, `WorktreeManager.test.ts` in that repo for fixture-driven contract testing style.

## Out of Scope

- **Langfuse observability**. Deferred to v2+. Tracked in ROADMAP.md.
- **Swarm mode (`--swarm N`)**. Deferred to v2+. Tracked in ROADMAP.md.
- **Extra agent providers (Qwen, Kimi, Aider, OpenCode)**. Deferred to v2+. Tracked in ROADMAP.md.
- **Docker/Podman sandbox providers**. v1 uses `noSandbox()` (agent runs directly on host). Deferred to v2+. Tracked in ROADMAP.md.
- **GitHub Issues tracker support**. The current bash supports both beads and GH Issues; v1 drops GH Issues.
- **Human-in-the-loop / ralph-once mode**. The current bash has `ralph-once.sh` for HITL single iterations; v1 drops it.
- **Migration from old `scripts/ralph/prompt.md` layout**. Users with old projects have to run `ralph init` and copy their prompt content manually.
- **`@claude` mentions in PRs**. The PR body is bare; tagging Claude on the PR is left to the user post-merge.

## Further Notes

- The `Codex` provider's invocation pattern is `codex exec --json --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox -m <model>` (lifted from sandcastle's reference implementation).
- The `Claude` provider's invocation pattern is `claude -p --output-format stream-json --verbose --dangerously-skip-permissions --model <model>`.
- The agent provider abstraction passes the prompt via stdin when the prompt is large enough to risk the Linux 128 KB per-arg limit (sandcastle's `PrintCommand.stdin` mechanism).
- The `ParsedStreamEvent` shape is: `{ type: "text", text } | { type: "result", result } | { type: "tool_call", name, args } | { type: "session_id", sessionId }`.
- `bd memories` is a real first-class command (`bd memories [search]`); the prompt's shell expression can leverage it directly without extra arguments.
- The user's existing `/quality-gate` skill wraps the `code-quality-verifier` subagent; `QualityGateRunner` invokes the skill rather than maintaining a parallel review prompt.
- The package.json `bin` field maps `ralph` to the built CLI entrypoint.
- The user prefers concise commit messages and semantic branch names per the linked gist (https://gist.github.com/seunggabi/87f8c722d35cd07deb3f649d45a31082).
- The terms used throughout this PRD are defined in `CONTEXT.md` at the repo root; ADRs in `docs/adr/` capture the architectural decisions.

### Unresolved at PRD time

The following questions were noted at the end of the grilling session and should be resolved during implementation (none blocks starting):

1. Verify `/tdd` and `/quality-gate` slash commands actually resolve in headless `claude -p` mode.
2. Determine CallMeBot's URL length cap empirically and pick a truncation threshold.
3. Decide PR base branch resolution rule (host's HEAD at invocation time vs `git merge-base`).
4. Decide where the cost pricing table lives (in-package JSON, refreshable, or remote-fetched).
5. Decide `bd dolt push` cadence (per iteration vs per invocation vs user-managed).
6. Decide whether `.ralph/ralph.config.json` accepts a `feedbackLoop: string[]` shortcut to bypass autodiscovery.
7. Decide a hard cap on `bd memories` output size to prevent prompt bloat.
8. Decide npm publish name (`ralph` vs scoped).
9. Decide the new repo's own `CLAUDE.md` content (Ralph being used on Ralph itself).
notes:
## Resolved Questions (2026-05-21)

Resolutions to the 9 "Unresolved at PRD time" items in the description:

1. **`/tdd` and `/quality-gate` in headless `claude -p`** — Assume they resolve from `~/.claude/skills/`. If Slice 6 (QualityGate) implementation finds they don't, fall back to inlining the skill content into the QG prompt and file a follow-up bead.

2. **CallMeBot URL length cap** — 1500-char URL-encoded budget. Truncate `Done:` task list to first 10 entries with `+N more` tail when over budget.

3. **PR base branch resolution** — Capture `git branch --show-current` at invocation start, store in state file, use as fixed base for the entire invocation. Cannot drift mid-run.

4. **Pricing table location** — In-package versioned JSON. Ships with each release. Refreshed via package version bumps. Works offline.

5. **`bd dolt push` cadence** — User-managed (no auto-push from Ralph). The user already pushes beads at session end; auto-pushing per iteration risks conflicts.

6. **`feedbackLoop: string[]` config shortcut** — Yes. Autodiscovery by default; explicit override in `.ralph/ralph.config.json` as escape hatch.

7. **`bd memories` output size cap** — 4KB hard cap with `...(N more)` truncation indicator.

8. **npm publish name / publishing strategy** — Deferred to v2+. Filed under "npm publishing + semantic-release CI" in ROADMAP.md. v1 ships as a local build (consumed via `pnpm link` or git install). Package metadata uses `@franco-albanese/ralph` as the eventual scoped name. **Toolchain: pnpm only, no npm.**

9. **New repo's `CLAUDE.md` content** — Brief: repo IS the Ralph CLI, build/test commands, beads tracker reference, session-close protocol. Don't pre-document architecture; let it grow from the code.

title: Ship v1: enable dogfood install via pnpm link
description:
v1 epic ralph-wiggum-claude-a84 (Ralph v1 TypeScript CLI rewrite) is functionally complete: iteration loop, init wizard, quality gate, stream display + cost, detached mode, WhatsApp notify, Codex provider, config + env loading. The only remaining gap to "I can use this from any project" is that the package is private at 0.0.0 with no install path.

Decision (grilled): no public npm. Dogfood-only via `pnpm link --global` from this repo. Reasoning lives in ADR 0003 (to be added). The link mechanism is the right fit because Ralph is still actively evolving — edit source, `pnpm build`, next invocation picks up the change.

Scope:
- README "Install (dogfood)" section: `pnpm install && pnpm build && pnpm link --global`, plus the manual `pnpm build` after edits caveat.
- docs/adr/0003-dogfood-install-via-pnpm-link.md, same terse shape as 0001/0002 — captures the no-public-npm trade-off so a future reader doesn't think `private: true` is an oversight.
- One Ralph invocation against a non-Ralph project of the author's choosing.

Closes the v1 epic on pass.
acceptance_criteria:
- README install section published, runnable from clean clone.
- ADR 0003 committed alongside 0001 and 0002.
- One Ralph invocation against a non-Ralph project produces (a) a draft PR with >=1 commit, (b) a WhatsApp message received at COMPLETE or stall, (c) no Ralph crashes during the run.
- Any rough edges observed during the dogfood run are filed as follow-up beads parented to ralph-wiggum-claude-a84 — they are NOT blockers for closing this bead.
- On pass: close this bead AND close ralph-wiggum-claude-a84.

title: QG: switch to native headless /quality-gate slash-command resolution
description: Today the QG runner inlines the skill markdown into its prompt because headless 'claude -p' slash-command resolution wasn't proven stable for /quality-gate. When headless slash resolution becomes reliable, swap spawnQualityGateAgent's positional -p prompt for an actual /quality-gate invocation and drop the inlined skill body. Keep the structured <ralph-qg>...</ralph-qg> output contract either way.

title: Sync AGENTS.md with CLAUDE.md configuration
description: AGENTS.md needs to include the same project configuration sections present in CLAUDE.md while preserving Codex-specific non-interactive shell guidance.
close_reason: AGENTS.md now includes the project configuration sections present in CLAUDE.md.

title: Open draft PR on first commit, not after loop exits
description:
## Why
PR #13's iteration loop currently calls pushBranch+createDraftPr only after runInvocation returns. The bead description for ralph-wiggum-claude-3oo specified 'draft after iter 1, ready after COMPLETE' for visibility on long/stalled runs. Literal AC list didn't include this, so it shipped — but CodeRabbit + audit both flagged it as a UX regression.

## What to build
- Wire a 'first-commit' hook through runInvocation (or callback) so pushBranch+createDraftPr fire once when commitsAhead > 0 mid-loop
- Guard against duplicate-PR creation
- Keep markPrReady gated on summary.outcome === 'complete' (unchanged)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Draft PR is created after the first iteration that produces commits, not after the loop exits
- [ ] Subsequent iterations do not re-create the PR
- [ ] markPrReady still only fires on outcome === 'complete'
- [ ] Test asserts ordering: createDraftPr called before iteration 2 in a 2-iteration scenario
- [ ] Stalled run with commits still ends with a draft PR (no orphaned work)

## Related
- PR #13 + ralph-wiggum-claude-3oo (the parent feature)
- CodeRabbit thread on src/run.ts:80-99
close_reason: implemented in PR #13 — draft PR now opens after first iteration that produces commits

title: ralph init wizard + bash script removal
description:
## Parent

ralph-wiggum-claude-a84

## What to build

`InitWizard` runs interactively: prompts for default agent, default model, max-iter, branch prefix list, beads ID prefix. Writes:

- `.ralph/ralph.config.json` (per Slice 9 schema)
- `.ralph/.env.example` with placeholders for `WHATSAPP_PHONE`, `WHATSAPP_APIKEY`, agent API keys
- `.ralph/prompt.md` from the bundled sandcastle-style template (numbered workflow, `{{KEY}}` placeholders filled, `\!`shell expressions`` pre-populated)
- Updates `.gitignore` to exclude `.ralph/.env`, `.ralph/state/`, `.ralph/logs/`, `.ralph/worktrees/`

Opens `.ralph/prompt.md` in `$EDITOR` at the end. Re-running `ralph init` in an existing project confirms before overwriting.

Same PR deletes the legacy bash artefacts: `afk-ralph.sh`, `ralph-once.sh`, `scripts/ralph/`. Repo's own `CLAUDE.md` updated with build/test commands + beads tracker reference (resolved PRD question #9 — brief, not architectural).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `ralph init` writes config, env example, prompt, and updates `.gitignore`
- [ ] Bundled prompt template uses sandcastle-style `{{KEY}}` and `\!`shell` expressions
- [ ] `$EDITOR` opens on the new prompt at end of wizard
- [ ] Re-running `ralph init` in an existing project confirms before overwriting
- [ ] `afk-ralph.sh`, `ralph-once.sh`, `scripts/ralph/` deleted in the same PR
- [ ] Repo's `CLAUDE.md` updated with build/test/beads instructions (brief)

## Blocked by

- ralph-wiggum-claude-jdp
- ralph-wiggum-claude-dhs

title: Detached mode + ralph status/tail/stop
description:
## Parent

ralph-wiggum-claude-a84

## What to build

`Daemonizer` spawns a detached child via `child_process.spawn({detached: true, stdio: 'ignore'})`, redirecting stdout/stderr to the log file. `StateStore` owns `.ralph/state/<pid>.json` lifecycle with atomic write-rename and the PRD state shape (pid, branch, agent, model, startedAt, iteration, currentBead, tasksDone, tokens, costUsd, logPath, prUrl).

CLI gains three subcommands:

- `ralph run --detach`: spawn detached, print pid + log path, parent exits immediately
- `ralph status`: list active runs (read all `.ralph/state/*.json`); detect + clean stale PIDs
- `ralph tail [<pid>]`: follow log file in real time; no PID picks most recent
- `ralph stop [<pid>]`: SIGTERM target; no PID picks only active run; ambiguous case errors

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `ralph run --detach` spawns a detached child that survives parent exit
- [ ] `.ralph/state/<pid>.json` written atomically; removed on clean exit
- [ ] `ralph status` lists active runs with required fields; cleans up stale PIDs
- [ ] `ralph tail` follows log file in real time; no PID picks most recent
- [ ] `ralph stop` SIGTERMs the target PID; ambiguous case errors with clear message
- [ ] Unit tests cover atomic write, stale-PID detection, and daemon smoke test

## Blocked by

- ralph-wiggum-claude-9ak

title: AgentProvider abstraction + Codex provider
description:
## Parent

ralph-wiggum-claude-a84

## What to build

Refactor the walking skeleton's hardcoded Claude invocation behind the `AgentProvider` interface: `name: string`, `env: Record<string,string>`, `buildPrintCommand(options): PrintCommand`, `parseStreamLine(line) => ParsedStreamEvent[]`, optional `parseSessionUsage(content): IterationUsage | undefined`. Implement two concrete providers:

- `claude(model)`: invokes `claude -p --output-format stream-json --verbose --dangerously-skip-permissions --model <m>`
- `codex(model)`: invokes `codex exec --json --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox -m <model>`

CLI gains `--agent claude|codex` flag (default `claude`). `ParsedStreamEvent` is the single normalised shape (`text | result | tool_call | session_id`) — no downstream branching on agent identity. Stream-JSON parsing covered by per-agent fixture contract tests using captured streams from real invocations against a tiny prompt.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `AgentProvider` interface defined per PRD shape
- [ ] `claude` and `codex` concrete providers implemented
- [ ] `--agent claude|codex` flag routes to correct provider; default is `claude`
- [ ] Both providers tested against captured fixture streams (committed under `tests/fixtures/`)
- [ ] Malformed JSON lines skipped silently
- [ ] Partial streams without completion event handled
- [ ] `ParsedStreamEvent` shape consistent across both providers

## Blocked by

- ralph-wiggum-claude-9ak

title: WhatsApp rich notify via CallMeBot
description:
## Parent

ralph-wiggum-claude-a84

## What to build

`WhatsAppNotifier` formats the rich plain-text message (CallMeBot can't render markdown) at terminal state (COMPLETE or stall): status (success/warning), project, branch, PR URL, iterations used/max + wall time, tasks done/blocked, tokens in/out, USD cost, one-line summary per task done, and the QG findings line. URL-encodes and POSTs to CallMeBot with a 10-second timeout. Best-effort — failures are logged but never throw upstream. Truncates `Done:` list to first 10 entries with `+N more` tail when URL-encoded body exceeds 1500 chars (resolved PRD question #2). Opt-in via `WHATSAPP_PHONE` + `WHATSAPP_APIKEY` in `.ralph/.env`; silently skip when missing.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] COMPLETE and STALL message variants render correctly per PRD format
- [ ] CallMeBot called with 10-second timeout
- [ ] Network failure or non-2xx response does NOT throw upstream
- [ ] `Done:` list truncated to 10 + `+N more` when over 1500 URL-encoded chars
- [ ] Missing `WHATSAPP_PHONE` / `WHATSAPP_APIKEY` silently skips notify
- [ ] Unit tests with fake `fetch` cover formatting + truncation + failure path

## Blocked by

- ralph-wiggum-claude-9ak
- ralph-wiggum-claude-u61

title: SIGINT graceful shutdown
description:
## Parent

ralph-wiggum-claude-a84

## What to build

First Ctrl-C sends SIGTERM to the agent subprocess, drains its stream for up to 30 seconds, prints a partial summary, and exits with code 130. Second Ctrl-C within 5 seconds sends SIGKILL and exits immediately. PR remains draft after shutdown. State file is removed. No WhatsApp notify fires on Ctrl-C exit. Worktree (from Slice 4) is still cleaned up.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] First SIGINT triggers SIGTERM to agent + 30s drain + partial summary + exit 130
- [ ] Second SIGINT within 5s triggers SIGKILL + immediate exit
- [ ] PR stays draft after Ctrl-C
- [ ] State file removed on Ctrl-C exit
- [ ] No WhatsApp call fires on Ctrl-C
- [ ] Worktree cleaned up after Ctrl-C

## Blocked by

- ralph-wiggum-claude-3oo

title: Quality gate at COMPLETE writes PR title/body and files follow-ups
description:
## Parent

ralph-wiggum-claude-a84

## What to build

`QualityGateRunner` runs at COMPLETE on the full PR diff (one invocation per run, not per iteration). Builds a QG prompt sourcing `git diff <base>..HEAD`, the list of touched beads, the active epic's notes, and `.claude/rules/*`. Spawns the QG agent via `AgentProvider`. Captures structured output: PR title (using branch's semantic prefix, e.g. `feat: ...`), 2-sentence body + bullet list of closed beads (no Ralph branding, no run metadata), and follow-up bead IDs. Auto-fixes high-severity findings in place; files ambiguous findings as open beads with the active epic as parent and a follow-up label. Calls `gh pr edit --title <X> --body <Y>` then `gh pr ready`.

Uses the user's existing `/quality-gate` skill (resolved PRD question #1). If headless slash command resolution fails during impl, fall back to inlining the skill content into the QG prompt and file a follow-up bead capturing the limitation.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] At COMPLETE, QG runs exactly once against the full PR diff
- [ ] PR title takes the semantic prefix from the branch
- [ ] PR body is 2 sentences + bullet list of closed beads, no Ralph metadata
- [ ] High-severity findings auto-fixed in place and committed
- [ ] Ambiguous findings filed as open beads (parent = active epic, follow-up label)
- [ ] `gh pr edit` then `gh pr ready` on success
- [ ] Headless `/quality-gate` invocation verified; fallback path implemented if resolution fails

## Blocked by

- ralph-wiggum-claude-3oo

title: Stream display + cost tracking + structured log
description:
## Parent

ralph-wiggum-claude-a84

## What to build

`StreamDisplay` renders normalised `ParsedStreamEvent`s to the terminal: streamed prose dimmed, tool calls as one-line `Bash: bd ready ...` entries with truncated args, styled iteration summary box between iterations (iteration N/max, task closed, tokens in/out, USD cost), final summary box at exit with cumulative totals. Uses `@clack/prompts` for styled output. `CostCalculator` reads the in-package versioned pricing JSON (per model x per-token type: input / output / cache-create / cache-read) and converts per-iteration `IterationUsage` events to USD. Token usage parsed from each agent's stream (no extra API call). Structured log always written to `.ralph/logs/<timestamp>-<pid>.log`, one JSON event per line.

Pricing JSON ships in-package per resolved PRD question #4. Refreshed via package version bumps.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Prose text streams dimmed in real time
- [ ] Tool calls render as one-line entries with truncated args
- [ ] Iteration summary box renders between iterations with iter#, task closed, tokens, cost
- [ ] Final summary box renders at exit with cumulative totals
- [ ] Pricing JSON ships in-package, versioned
- [ ] Unknown model falls back to zero cost + warning
- [ ] `.ralph/logs/<timestamp>-<pid>.log` contains one JSON event per line
- [ ] Unit tests cover cost calculation, summary rendering, and log shape

## Blocked by

- ralph-wiggum-claude-3oo

title: Config + secrets loader with Zod validation
description:
## Parent

ralph-wiggum-claude-a84

## What to build

`ConfigLoader` loads `.ralph/ralph.config.json` and `.ralph/.env`, merges with CLI flags (CLI wins), validates against a Zod schema. Schema covers `defaultAgent`, `defaultModel`, `maxIter`, `branchPrefixes`, `completionSignal`, and `feedbackLoop: string[]` override (resolved PRD question #6 — autodiscovery default, explicit override as escape hatch). Validation errors surface with full JSON path + suggestion. Secrets live only in `.env` (CallMeBot phone + apikey, agent API keys); config is committed-safe.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `.ralph/ralph.config.json` + `.ralph/.env` parse and merge correctly
- [ ] CLI flags override config values
- [ ] Zod validation surfaces full JSON path + suggestion on error
- [ ] `feedbackLoop: string[]` override bypasses autodiscovery when present
- [ ] Missing optional fields fall back to documented defaults
- [ ] Unit tests cover valid + invalid configs and override precedence

## Blocked by

- ralph-wiggum-claude-yrs

title: Worktree isolation per invocation
description:
## Parent

ralph-wiggum-claude-a84

## What to build

`WorktreeManager` creates a git worktree under `.ralph/worktrees/<slugified-branch>/` with the source branch attached. The agent runs inside the worktree (cwd = worktree path). On clean exit OR failure OR Ctrl-C, the worktree is removed and the branch reference cleaned up. `--branch` is validated against the semantic prefix list: `feat/`, `fix/`, `chore/`, `docs/`, `refactor/`, `perf/`, `test/`, `style/`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `ralph run --branch feat/x` creates `.ralph/worktrees/feat-x/`
- [ ] Agent subprocess runs with cwd = worktree path
- [ ] Worktree removed on clean exit
- [ ] Worktree removed on agent crash or Ctrl-C
- [ ] Invalid branch prefix (e.g. `--branch random/foo`) rejected with a clear error
- [ ] Integration tests against a temp git repo cover create + attach + cleanup-on-failure

## Blocked by

- ralph-wiggum-claude-yrs

title: Prompt template with {{KEY}} substitution and shell expressions
description:
## Parent

ralph-wiggum-claude-a84

## What to build

`PromptPreprocessor` reads `.ralph/prompt.md`, runs `{{KEY}}` substitution first (built-ins `BRANCH`, `TARGET_BRANCH` — non-overridable), then expands ``\!`shell command` `` lines into their stdout. Unmatched `{{KEY}}` is a fatal error before iteration 1. Shell-expression failures are visible (logged + inlined with an error marker) rather than silent. The bundled prompt's `bd memories` expression output is hard-capped at 4KB with `...(N more)` truncation (resolved PRD question #7).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `{{BRANCH}}` and `{{TARGET_BRANCH}}` substitute correctly; user cannot override them
- [ ] Unmatched `{{KEY}}` aborts the run before iteration 1 with a clear error
- [ ] Shell expressions (`\!`bd ready``, `\!`git log``, etc.) execute and inline their stdout
- [ ] Shell command failures surface in the rendered prompt as an error marker (not silent)
- [ ] `bd memories` expression output truncates at 4KB with `...(N more)` indicator
- [ ] Unit tests cover substitution + expansion + failure paths

## Blocked by

- ralph-wiggum-claude-yrs

title: Iteration loop + completion detection + max-iter + timeout
description:
## Parent

ralph-wiggum-claude-a84

## What to build

Wrap the walking-skeleton single-shot invocation in an iteration loop. Each iteration spawns a fresh agent context (no conversation history). `CompletionDetector` scans the normalised stream for `<promise>COMPLETE</promise>` (default) or a custom `--complete-signal <regex>`. Loop exits when the signal matches OR `--max-iter` (default 10) is reached OR the per-iteration `--timeout-min` (default 30) fires. On COMPLETE the PR is marked ready; on stall the PR stays draft.

Agent crashes (non-zero exit) are logged as iteration failures and the loop moves on; if >50% of iterations crash, the invocation aborts as a stall.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Loop spawns a fresh agent subprocess each iteration (no shared session)
- [ ] `<promise>COMPLETE</promise>` in agent output ends the loop on the matching iteration
- [ ] `--max-iter` and `--timeout-min` flags override defaults (10, 30)
- [ ] Per-iteration timeout SIGTERMs the agent and proceeds to the next iteration
- [ ] PR marked `gh pr ready` only on COMPLETE; remains draft on stall
- [ ] `--complete-signal '<regex>'` overrides the default detection pattern
- [ ] Crash rate >50% aborts the invocation as a stall

## Blocked by

- ralph-wiggum-claude-yrs

title: Walking skeleton: ralph run end-to-end
description:
## Parent

ralph-wiggum-claude-a84

## What to build

The minimal viable `ralph` CLI: a TypeScript binary that, given `ralph run --branch feat/<name>`, spawns Claude Code once in the current directory, streams its text to stdout, pushes the resulting commits, opens a draft PR against the captured base branch, and exits. No worktree, no iteration loop, no completion detection, no cost tracking, no agent abstraction yet — just the end-to-end pipe proving the CLI can produce a real PR via a subprocess-wrapped Claude.

Toolchain is pnpm-only (no npm). Package name is `@franco-albanese/ralph` but publishing is deferred to v2+ (tracked in ROADMAP.md) — local builds + `pnpm link` only. Base branch is captured via `git branch --show-current` at invocation start and stored for the entire run.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pnpm install && pnpm build` produces a working `ralph` binary via the package's `bin` field
- [ ] `ralph run --branch feat/smoke-test` spawns `claude -p --output-format stream-json --verbose --dangerously-skip-permissions` in cwd
- [ ] Agent text content streams to stdout in real time
- [ ] After agent exits with commits, a draft PR is opened against the captured base branch via `gh pr create --draft`
- [ ] CLI exits cleanly with the PR URL printed
- [ ] No npm references anywhere in tooling (lockfile, scripts, README)

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately
close_reason: Walking skeleton merged in PR #8. ralph run --branch <name> captures base, spawns claude, streams text, opens draft PR via gh.

title: Remove bash-era leftovers (templates/, notify.env.example)
description:
The repo still has bash-era artifacts that aren't referenced by the TS CLI:
- templates/prompt-beads.md, templates/prompt-github.md (TS init wizard embeds its own templates in src/init/template.ts)
- templates/notify.env.example (TS notify reads from .ralph/.env per src/config/env-file.ts)

Verify nothing in src/ or scripts/ references them, then delete. Follow-up surfaced while rewriting README for the v1 ship slice (ralph-wiggum-claude-8vm).

title: ralph cleanup command for retained branches
description:
## Why

PR #15 changed WorktreeManager.remove() to preserve the branch reference instead of force-deleting it. The rationale is sound (the branch holds agent commits; deleting it before they're confirmed on origin destroys work). The trade-off, by design, is that local branches now accumulate after every ralph run — there is no cleanup path.

For power users this becomes a real ergonomic problem within a few dozen invocations: stale branches clutter `git branch` output, and any `ralph run --branch <same-name>` after a prior run will fail with a raw git error because the branch already exists.

## What to build

`ralph cleanup` subcommand that lists and optionally deletes branches created by prior ralph runs whose commits are already on origin (i.e., safely pushed and merged).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `ralph cleanup` (no args) lists candidate branches: those whose tip is reachable from `origin/<base>` (i.e., merged) or whose content matches a squash-merged commit on origin
- [ ] `ralph cleanup --branch <name>` targets a specific branch
- [ ] `ralph cleanup --apply` actually deletes (default is dry-run for safety)
- [ ] Refuses to delete branches with unpushed commits unless `--force`
- [ ] Refuses to delete a branch currently checked out in any worktree
- [ ] Unit tests cover: merged-via-fast-forward, squash-merged (content-equivalent), unpushed-commits-refused, currently-checked-out-refused

## Related

- ralph-wiggum-claude-2yb (worktree isolation slice — original context)
- Surfaced by quality-gate audit on PR #15

title: Unregister SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers on clean ralph run exit
description: installSignalAbort in src/run.ts registers SIGINT+SIGTERM listeners via process.once and only removes them when a signal fires. On the clean-exit path the listeners stay attached. Harmless for the one-shot CLI but should be removed in a finally before the iteration loop slice (3oo) introduces longer-lived state.

title: Adopt Effect TS for backend runtime in Ralph CLI
description: Project rule (.claude/rules/backend/typescript/apps.md) requires Effect TS for backend application logic. The walking skeleton and worktree-isolation slices use plain async/Promise idioms. Migrate WorktreeManager + run.ts to Effect once the surface area justifies it (likely after the iteration loop and config loader slices land).

value: Slice-implementation agent PR lifecycle: (1) open PR as DRAFT during implementation via 'gh pr create --draft'. (2) Implement + run quality gate. (3) Address every quality-gate finding and push the fixes. (4) Mark the PR ready for review via 'gh pr ready <n>' — this is the agent's terminal step. (5) DO NOT close the bead yet — the bead stays in_progress (or a 'ready-for-review' label) until the PR actually MERGES. (6) Bead is closed only after the PR has landed in main, with 'bd close <id> --reason="merged via PR #n"' followed by 'bd dolt push'. Rationale: closing the bead at PR-open time creates false-positive 'done' signals — work isn't done until it's merged. Apply this in any agent prompt that spawns parallel vertical-slice work.
Repo maintainability rule: keep every source and test file at or under 300 lines. If a file would exceed that, split it into cohesive modules/tests before finishing.
